title: io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder: .reset() writes self->seennl (textio.c) with no lock vs .newlines reading it -- = cpython#144777 (CLOSED)
description: The _io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder keeps its seen-newline bitmask in a plain self->seennl field. _io_IncrementalNewlineDecoder_reset_impl (Modules/_io/textio.c:630) writes self->seennl = 0 with no critical section, while the `newlines` getter (incrementalnewlinedecoder_newlines_get, textio.c:644) reads self->seennl, and decode() reads/updates it (:389/:511). A shared decoder driven by .reset()/.decode() from one thread and .newlines from another is a data race on seennl. This IS cpython#144777 ('Possible data race in _io_IncrementalNewlineDecoder_reset_impl in textio.c', CLOSED).
race_kind: unsynchronized incremental-decoder state: a shared _io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder's seennl bitmask read/written across threads with no per-object critical section. Same class as the cjkcodecs incremental codec state (TSAN-0001).
shared_state: nldecoder_object.seennl (seen-newline bitmask) and pendingcr on a single shared IncrementalNewlineDecoder
severity: low (value-benign: a stale/mixed seennl bitmask -> a wrong .newlines report or a mis-split \r\n boundary under concurrency; no memory unsafety). Already filed as cpython#144777 (CLOSED).
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads sharing one io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder, half .reset() half reading .newlines) exits 66 on debug-ft-nojit-tsan. Fleet-12 surfaced it (1 veh).
suggested_fix: Take the decoder's per-object critical section (Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)) around the seennl/pendingcr read-modify-writes in reset/decode and the newlines getter, or make seennl atomic. (See cpython#144777.)
prior_art: cpython#144777 ('Possible data race in _io_IncrementalNewlineDecoder_reset_impl in textio.c', CLOSED) is exactly this race. Our build still reproduces it (either closed-as-wontfix, or the build predates a fix).
related: Incremental-decoder-state class, like TSAN-0001 (cjkcodecs MultibyteIncrementalDecoder / MultibyteStreamReader). Surfaced via --tsan-no-halt (fleet-12).
notes: Known (= cpython#144777, CLOSED). Cataloged for dedup. Value-benign. Not a new filing.
